**Author:** AJ Cassata
**Video title:** Are you ready for AI-Powered Outbound Sales? with AJ Cassata at Revenue Boost
**Channel/show:** Growth Think Tank
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2SuyVmT6GcY
**Upload date:** 2025-12-29
**Transcript source:** YouTube auto-generated English captions (via yt-dlp) — summarized, not reproduced verbatim, due to copyright
**Playbook relevance:** AI-in-the-loop workflow chapter — the most structured single source on this topic in the collection

---

## Summary

- **The 10-80-10 rule:** Cassata's framework for AI use — a human sets up the task (first 10%), AI does the bulk of the work (middle 80%), a human reviews/edits before it goes out (final 10%). He calls AI "a collaborator, not an employee" and warns against fully outsourcing any task to it because it "always going to hallucinate" eventually.
- **Email vs. LinkedIn tradeoffs:** LinkedIn gets far higher response rates (prospects can verify you via your profile/history) but is capped at roughly 100–150 outreach messages/week by the platform; email has a lower response rate but effectively unlimited volume. His practical split: use LinkedIn for your best/warmest target accounts, email for volume across everyone else.
- **Clay.com AI-agent workflow:** Describes building AI agents inside Clay that research each lead (e.g., checking whether a company runs Facebook ads) and insert findings into an email template as personalized variables — same idea as merge-tags for first names, but for whole researched sentences.
- **Common mistakes:** (1) Not sending enough volume — many clients "auto" a campaign after only ~200 emails; (2) Product-first messaging instead of leading with the prospect's problem; (3) Failing to segment large lists by role/industry/company size, since a CEO and a VP of Marketing respond to different language and priorities.
- **AI cold-calling:** Sees it as viable today only for simple tasks (appointment confirmations, basic qualification), not for actually running complex enterprise sales conversations.
- **Five levers of every campaign:** list, copy, offer, volume, and (for email specifically) deliverability — his framework for diagnosing why a campaign isn't working.

## Notable quotes

> "I look at it as a collaborator, not an employee... it's dangerous to fully outsource something 100% to AI because we all know that it makes mistakes and it hallucinates."

> "It's more work to do this, but that's also definitely worth the time" — on segmenting lists by role/industry rather than running one blanket campaign.
